Since each student attempted exactly 14 out of 15 questions, the number of students who attempted exactly one question is the number of students who did not attempt any of the 15 questions. 

Given that the sum of all \( a_{ij} \) is 972, we can use the principle of inclusion-exclusion to find the number of students who took the test. The principle of inclusion-exclusion states that the total number of students who attempted at least one question is the sum of the number of students who attempted each individual question minus the sum of the number of students who attempted each pair of questions plus the sum of the number of students who attempted each triplet of questions, and so on.

However, since each student attempted exactly 14 out of 15 questions, the number of students who attempted exactly one question is the number of students who did not attempt any of the 15 questions. This can be calculated as follows:

1. Calculate the total number of students who attempted at least one question.
2. Subtract the number of students who attempted exactly one question from the total number of students.
